question: Calculate the ultimate and allowable bearing capacity of a 2.5 m wide continuous strip footing founded 1.0 m below grade in sand with friction angle 34 degrees, unit weight 19 kN/m3, and zero cohesion. Use a factor of safety of 3. Show the key numbers.

Calculate the ultimate and allowable bearing capacity of a 3 m by 3 m square footing founded 1.5 m below grade in saturated clay under undrained conditions with su = 75 kPa and unit weight 18 kN/m3. Use a factor of safety of 3.

A 2 m by 4 m rectangular footing carries a vertical load with a moment producing an eccentricity of 0.3 m along the long axis. It is founded 1.0 m deep in sand, phi = 32 degrees, gamma = 19 kN/m3. Compute the bearing capacity using the effective-area method.
A 2 m square footing is founded 1.0 m deep on 1.5 m of stiff clay (su = 100 kPa) overlying soft clay (su = 30 kPa). Evaluate the bearing capacity accounting for the weaker underlying layer.

Estimate the primary consolidation settlement of a 3 m square footing bearing 150 kPa net, over a 4 m thick normally consolidated clay layer (Cc = 0.25, e0 = 0.9) whose top is 2 m below the footing base. Water table at the ground surface, clay unit weight 18 kN/m3.

Estimate the consolidation settlement of a 5 m thick overconsolidated clay layer (OCR = 2, Cc = 0.30, Cr = 0.05, e0 = 0.8, preconsolidation pressure 120 kPa) under a uniform added vertical stress of 60 kPa.
Estimate the immediate (elastic) settlement of a 2.5 m square rigid footing bearing 200 kPa on a deep sand deposit with Young's modulus E = 25 MPa and Poisson's ratio 0.3.
Estimate the time required to reach 90 percent primary consolidation for a 4 m thick doubly-drained clay layer with coefficient of consolidation cv = 3 m2/yr.
Estimate the ultimate axial compression capacity of a 0.4 m diameter, 15 m long driven concrete pile in sand with friction angle 32 degrees and unit weight 19 kN/m3, water table at 3 m depth.

Estimate the ultimate axial compression capacity of a 0.45 m diameter, 18 m long driven concrete pile in clay with undrained shear strength su = 60 kPa and unit weight 18 kN/m3, using the alpha method.
Provide a table of ultimate axial capacity versus embedded depth, from 5 m to 20 m, for a 0.4 m diameter driven pile in sand with phi = 33 degrees and unit weight 19 kN/m3.
Estimate the ultimate uplift (tension) capacity of a 0.4 m diameter, 15 m long driven pile in sand with phi = 32 degrees and unit weight 19 kN/m3, water table at 3 m.
Estimate the side and base resistance of a 1.0 m diameter drilled shaft, 12 m deep in clay with su = 80 kPa, using the GEC-10 alpha method.
Estimate the ultimate axial capacity of a 1.2 m diameter, 15 m deep drilled shaft in sand with phi = 34 degrees and unit weight 19 kN/m3, using the beta method.
Estimate the side and tip resistance of a 1.0 m diameter drilled shaft socketed 3 m into rock with unconfined compressive strength qu = 10 MPa.

Estimate the groundline deflection and maximum bending moment of a free-head 0.5 m diameter steel pipe pile embedded 10 m in sand (phi = 34 degrees, gamma = 19 kN/m3) under a 100 kN lateral load applied at the ground surface.
Repeat the lateral analysis of the 0.5 m pile in sand under 100 kN, but for a fixed-head (restrained against rotation) condition, and compare deflection and moment to the free-head case.
Estimate the axial group capacity and group efficiency of a 3 by 3 group of 0.4 m diameter driven piles at 1.2 m center-to-center spacing in clay with su = 60 kPa.
For a 2 by 3 pile group of 0.4 m piles at 1.2 m spacing carrying a vertical load of 4000 kN plus a lateral load of 400 kN, estimate the load distribution to individual piles.
Determine the required embedment depth of a cantilever sheet pile wall retaining 4 m of sand (phi = 32 degrees, gamma = 18 kN/m3) with the water table at the base of the excavation.
Design an anchored sheet pile wall for a 6 m retained height in sand (phi = 32 degrees, gamma = 18 kN/m3) with a tie-back anchor 1.5 m below the top: find the embedment depth and the anchor force.
Estimate the strut loads for an 8 m deep braced excavation in soft to medium clay (su = 40 kPa, gamma = 18 kN/m3) using an apparent earth pressure diagram, with three strut levels.
Design a cantilever soldier-pile-and-lagging wall for a 4 m deep excavation in sand (phi = 33 degrees, gamma = 18 kN/m3): required embedment and maximum bending moment.
Compute the factor of safety against basal heave for a 7 m deep, 10 m wide excavation in clay with su = 35 kPa and unit weight 17 kN/m3.
Develop a bearing graph (ultimate capacity versus blow count) for a 0.4 m square precast concrete pile driven in sand with a hammer of rated energy 50 kJ.
Estimate the driving blow count required to reach an ultimate capacity of 800 kN for a 0.4 m square pile in sand using the wave equation.
For a site with average shear wave velocity Vs30 = 240 m/s, determine the NEHRP / ASCE 7 site class and briefly explain its design implications.

Compute the Mononobe-Okabe seismic active earth pressure on a 5 m high retaining wall with cohesionless backfill (phi = 34 degrees, gamma = 18 kN/m3), horizontal seismic coefficient kh = 0.3, vertical coefficient kv = 0.

Evaluate liquefaction triggering at 6 m depth: corrected SPT (N1)60 = 12, total vertical stress 110 kPa, effective vertical stress 70 kPa, moment magnitude Mw = 7.0, peak ground acceleration 0.3g. Report the factor of safety against triggering.

Check a cantilever concrete retaining wall with a 4.5 m stem retaining cohesionless backfill (phi = 32 degrees, gamma = 18 kN/m3) for sliding, overturning, and bearing. Assume a reasonable base width and report factors of safety.
Evaluate the external stability (sliding, overturning, bearing) of a 6 m high MSE wall with reinforced fill at phi = 34 degrees and retained fill at phi = 30 degrees, per GEC-11.
Compute the active earth pressure on a 5 m high wall with backfill phi = 30 degrees, wall friction delta = 20 degrees, and a backfill sloping at 10 degrees, comparing Rankine and Coulomb results.
Design aggregate piers to support a 3 m square footing bearing 250 kPa over soft clay (su = 25 kPa): estimate the settlement reduction and recommended pier spacing/diameter.
Determine the wick (prefabricated vertical) drain spacing needed to achieve 90 percent consolidation within 6 months for a 5 m thick clay layer with cv = 2 m2/yr under a surcharge.

Search for the critical circular slip surface and report the minimum factor of safety for a 10 m high slope cut at 2H:1V in clay with c = 20 kPa, phi = 24 degrees, unit weight 19 kN/m3, with no water table.
Find the factor of safety of an 8 m high slope at 1.5H:1V with a phreatic surface at mid-height, drained strength c' = 10 kPa, phi' = 28 degrees, unit weight 20 kN/m3.
Evaluate a two-layer slope: 3 m of fill (c = 5 kPa, phi = 30 degrees) over clay (c = 25 kPa, phi = 20 degrees), 9 m total height at 2H:1V. Find the critical surface and factor of safety.

Determine the neutral plane location and the downdrag (drag) load on a 0.4 m diameter driven pile passing through 8 m of consolidating clay (su = 30 kPa) into dense sand, with a 40 kPa surcharge applied at the surface.
Estimate the downdrag force per UFC 3-220-20 on a pile embedded in 6 m of settling fill (gamma = 19 kN/m3) over competent bearing soil.
Compute the ASCE 7-22 design wind force on a 3 m tall, 30 m long ground-mounted solid freestanding wall, basic wind speed V = 115 mph, Exposure C.
Classify a soil with 65 percent passing the No. 200 sieve, liquid limit 45, plasticity index 22, by both the USCS and AASHTO systems.

From a raw SPT blow count N = 18 measured at 4 m depth with effective vertical stress 70 kPa and hammer efficiency 60 percent, compute the corrected (N1)60 and estimate an allowable bearing capacity for a shallow footing.

Perform a CPT-based liquefaction triggering evaluation (Boulanger and Idriss 2014) for a layer at 5 m depth: cone tip resistance qc = 4 MPa, sleeve friction fs = 40 kPa, total vertical stress 95 kPa, effective vertical stress 60 kPa, moment magnitude 7.5, peak ground acceleration 0.35g. Report the factor of safety.

Compute the reliability index using FORM for a foundation where resistance R is normally distributed with mean 1000 kN and standard deviation 150 kN, and load S is normally distributed with mean 600 kN and standard deviation 90 kN, for the limit state R minus S.

Set up a Sobol sensitivity analysis of bearing capacity with friction angle in [28, 36] degrees, unit weight in [17, 20] kN/m3, and footing width in [1.5, 3] m. Which input contributes most to the variance?

Run a one-dimensional site response analysis for a 30 m soil column with a shear wave velocity profile of 200, 300, and 400 m/s in three 10 m layers, subjected to an input peak ground acceleration of 0.3g. Describe required inputs and report the surface response.

Perform a one-dimensional equivalent-linear site response analysis for a 30 m soil column (Vs = 250 m/s, Darendeli modulus reduction and damping curves) with an input motion peak ground acceleration of 0.2g. Report surface peak ground acceleration and amplification factor.
Calibrate an MKZ nonlinear backbone (modulus reduction) curve for a sand whose small-strain shear modulus is derived from Vs = 220 m/s and unit weight 19 kN/m3.

What earthquake signal-processing analyses can you perform on an acceleration time history (for example response spectra, Arias intensity, significant duration)? Demonstrate on a simple synthetic input if possible.

Use a 2D plane-strain finite element analysis with strength reduction to estimate the bearing capacity (collapse load and factor of safety) of a 2 m wide strip footing on clay with su = 50 kPa and unit weight 18 kN/m3.
Use a 2D finite element strength reduction analysis to find the factor of safety of a 10 m high slope at 2H:1V in soil with c = 15 kPa, phi = 25 degrees, unit weight 19 kN/m3.

Use a 2D explicit finite difference (FLAC-style) analysis to estimate the settlement under a 5 m high embankment (gamma = 20 kN/m3) on a soft clay foundation with Young's modulus 10 MPa. Describe the model setup and report results.
Given SPT N values at four points (0,0)=10, (10,0)=14, (0,10)=9, (10,10)=13, fit a variogram and use kriging to estimate the value at (5,5).

Build a soil profile with 0 to 3 m of sand (gamma = 18 kN/m3), 3 to 8 m of clay (gamma = 17 kN/m3, su = 40 kPa), and the water table at 2 m. Report the total and effective vertical stress at 6 m depth.
I have a GEF-format CPT sounding file. What can you extract and summarize from it, and what inputs do you need from me to parse it?
What does your AGS4 reader validate, and what would you return from an AGS4 file containing borehole location and SPT data?
Outline the validation checks you run on a DIGGS 2.6 XML file, including schema and dictionary validation.
I have a DXF cross-section drawing of a slope. What layers and geometry can you discover, and how do you convert them into a slope stability model?
Export a cross-section to DXF given a ground surface defined by points (0,0), (10,5), (20,5) and a water table 1 m below the surface. What does the exported drawing include?
I have a PDF of a geotechnical cross-section. What geometry can you extract using the vector method versus the vision method, and what are the limitations of each?
What does an HVSR (horizontal-to-vertical spectral ratio) analysis from ambient noise tell me about a site, and what input data do you need to run it?
Explain a MASW surface-wave dispersion analysis and the inputs needed to derive a shear wave velocity profile.
Given tabular subsurface data with SPT N values at several depths across multiple borings, what visualizations and trend statistics can you produce?
Using NAVFAC DM7 guidance, what is the equation for the bearing capacity factor Nq, and what is its value for a friction angle of 30 degrees?
Per GEC-10 (FHWA drilled shaft guidance), describe the alpha method for unit side resistance in cohesive soils and the typical range of the alpha factor.
Per GEC-11, summarize the external stability checks required for the design of an MSE retaining wall.
What is the FHWA approach for grout-to-ground bond strength in micropile design, and what are typical bond values for a Type B micropile in sand?
Search the reference library for guidance on downdrag and negative skin friction on deep foundations and summarize what you find.

Using the GEC-10 (FHWA-NHI-18-024) alpha method, determine the alpha factor for drilled shaft side resistance in a clay with undrained shear strength su = 110 kPa (take atmospheric pressure pa = 101.3 kPa), then compute the unit side resistance and the total side shear capacity for a 1.2 m diameter shaft embedded 9 m in that clay. Cite the GEC-10 equation you used.

A 3 by 3 drilled shaft group is spaced at 3 shaft diameters center-to-center in the direction of loading. Using the p-multipliers recommended by GEC-10 (FHWA-NHI-18-024), state the lead-row and trailing-row p-multipliers and estimate the average lateral group efficiency. Cite the GEC-10 table you used.

Per GEC-8 (FHWA-HIF-07-03), estimate the unit shaft resistance of a drilled displacement (DD) pile in granular soil using the NeSmith correlation for an average SPT N60 of 22, stating the equation and any limiting value. Then give the allowable shaft capacity for a 0.45 m diameter, 12 m long pile using the GEC-8 ASD factor of safety with static load testing.

Following GEC-4 (FHWA-IF-99-015) for a tieback wall in sand (phi = 34 deg, gamma = 19 kN/m3, H = 9 m), give the apparent earth pressure (AEP) diagram peak pressure for sand and the resulting total horizontal load per unit width of wall. Also state the GEC-4 anchor proof-test load and the creep-test acceptance criteria.

Using the FHWA micropile reference (FHWA-NHI-05-039), select an ultimate grout-to-ground bond strength for a Type B micropile in medium dense to dense sand, then size the bond (load transfer) length required for a 350 kN design load using a factor of safety of 2.0 and a 175 mm drill hole diameter. State the bond value and the source table.

Using NAVFAC DM7, determine the Rankine active and passive lateral earth pressure coefficients for a vertical wall with horizontal cohesionless backfill at phi = 33 deg, then compute the total active and passive thrusts on a 6 m high wall with gamma = 18.5 kN/m3 and no water table. Cite the DM7 basis for the coefficients.

Using GEC-5 (FHWA-NHI-16-072) guidance, estimate the undrained shear strength su of a clay from a CPT cone resistance qt = 2.8 MPa at a depth where the total vertical stress is 130 kPa, choosing and stating a representative Nkt. Then apply the SHANSEP relationship from GEC-5 (give S and m) to estimate su for the same clay at an OCR of 4.

Per GEC-9 (FHWA-HIF-18-031), identify the recommended p-y model and p-multiplier values for a laterally loaded single pile in stiff clay above the water table, and the group p-multipliers at 3-diameter spacing. Summarize how these reduce the group's lateral resistance relative to a single pile.
